intentional_test_features: Anchoring / premature-closure trap: the acute exertional dyspnoea is easy to attribute wholly to body habitus and mechanical joint failure, but immobility plus class III obesity are VTE risk factors, so pulmonary embolism and ACS cannot be excluded remotely and must be safety-netted and referred for same-day in-person assessment.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present out-of-hours clinician who examined the joints, measured vitals, and administered oral tramadol. The AI Doctor cannot examine, measure clinician vitals without a device, obtain ECG/D-dimer/troponin/imaging, or initiate an opioid remotely; it is scored on pattern recognition + urgent in-person referral + escalation, not on prescribing.

T4/T5 ceiling discipline: the source's own Risk Level is Moderate (environmental/social) and its disposition is an out-of-hours assessment plus routine 7-day GP review; the patient is stable at rest (exertional-only dyspnoea that settles with rest, afebrile, stable HR, no chest pain/haemoptysis). Correct telehealth tier is a T3 uplift (urgent same-day in-person), NOT a T4/T5 000 call, unless a patient-reportable time-critical feature (rest dyspnoea, chest pain, haemoptysis, collapse) appears.

No-dose discipline: tramadol 50 mg (and any NSAID / GLP-1 / bariatric-surgery decision) is the clinician's answer, sealed in node 12 as should_NOT_recommend / not_indicated_here for the AI — an opioid initiated over telehealth in an obese patient with likely OSA is an error of commission.

Multi-morbidity load: class III obesity with end-stage knee osteoarthritis, obesity-related exertional dyspnoea / likely obstructive sleep apnoea, GORD, acanthosis nigricans (insulin resistance) and intertrigo.
Source category: EMD (Endocrine & Metabolic Disorders), source note EMD-030.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
